sentence: Subjective:
The patient, a 67-year-old right-handed male, presented with a complaint of a right shoulder injury that occurred approximately 4 days ago during a tackle football game.
He reports that he landed on his shoulder in an awkward position, which has been bothering him since then.
He denies any numbness or tingling in his fingers and has been able to grab objects, but has had difficulty lifting heavy objects.
He reports pain on the back side of his right shoulder, specifically around the acromioclavicular joint.
He has taken Tylenol for the pain, but it has not provided significant relief.
He denies any fever, chills, headache, or neck problems.
He has a history of appendicitis with an appendectomy 3-4 years ago.
Review of systems:
The patient denies any other joint problems, such as elbow or wrist pain, and has not had any other orthopedic injuries or surgeries.
Past medical history:
The patient has a history of appendicitis with an appendectomy 3-4 years ago.
Current medications:
I prescribed Tylenol for pain relief.
Objective:
Vital signs:
The patient's blood pressure is 122/76, pulse rate is 82 beats per minute, and oxygen saturation is 95% on room air.
Physical exam:

On examination, there is tenderness to palpation of the posterior shoulder at the acromioclavicular joint, decreased abduction and adduction to about 90 degrees, pain with supination and pronation of the right arm, and a palpable radial artery pulse.
Test results:
The patient had a right shoulder x-ray, which showed an acute acromioclavicular joint fracture.
Assessment / Problem List:
The patient has an acute acromioclavicular joint fracture with pain and limited mobility.
Problem list: Acromioclavicular joint fracture; confirmed.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15 milligrams once a day for pain relief.
I ordered a CT scan of the right shoulder to further evaluate the joint.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the shoulder and prevent future surgery.
I instructed the patient to use a sling for now and avoid heavy lifting or tackle football.
Follow-up:
The patient will follow up with me in a few days to review the CT scan results and progress.
